title: Dedicated Beta - Staged Rollouts check-in (gates + Console feedback)
preview: Beta check-in with Lattice AI on Dedicated staged rollouts. text: Beta check-in with Lattice AI on Dedicated staged rollouts. Customer likes the stage timeline concept and promotion audit trail, but reported friction around (1) gate strictness (p99 latency + 429/drop thresholds) and noisy failures during low-traffic periods, (2) confusion about what "saturation/headroom" means per stage, and (3) Console UX gaps: unclear gate evidence, limited per-region breakdown, and too many clicks to promote/hold/rollback. Redwood aligned on tuning: minimum sample size, better missing-metrics messaging, and UI improvements (gate evidence panel, annotations, and simplified promote confirmation).

title: Dedicated upgrade safety – staged rollouts / gates / rollback semantics (technical deep dive)
preview: Lattice AI asked for a detailed walkthrough of how Redwood will do safer Dedicated pool upgrades using staged rollouts. Redwood described the rollout plan model (stages, gates, promote/abort/hold) and emphasized deterministic, auditable actions with automatic abort/rollback on gate failure. The group focused on how ...
text: Lattice AI asked for a detailed walkthrough of how Redwood will do safer Dedicated pool upgrades using staged rollouts. Redwood described the rollout plan model (stages, gates, promote/abort/hold) and emphasized deterministic, auditable actions with automatic abort/rollback on gate failure. The group focused on how stages map to reserved capacity (traffic splitting vs replica partitions), avoiding starving small pools during canary, and how to handle missing/noisy metrics. Lattice AI emphasized strict change management (audit logs, RBAC, reason codes), and asked about rollback semantics for streaming and multi-region behavior if one region degrades mid-stage.
